title: Centaurea gabrieliae
intro: Centaurea gabrieliae, commonly known as Gabriel's Spurwort, is a perennial plant species native to the Balkan Peninsula, characterized by its purple flowers and taproot. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Centaurea gabrieliae include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used to prepare herbal infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Centaurea gabrieliae is classified within the family Asteraceae. It is a species of thistle native to the island of Sardinia. This plant belongs to the genus Centaurea, which comprises over 600 species. The exact classification may vary depending on the source, but it is commonly placed in the section Centaurea.
